name: Las Chorreras de San Martín del Pimpollar Trail
name_original: San Martín del Pimpollar — circular por las Chorreras de San Martín del Pimpollar
description: Hike the moderate Las Chorreras de San Martín del Pimpollar Trail to discover waterfalls, bridges, and stunning Sierra de Gredos views.
region_intro: Hiking around San Martín Del Pimpollar offers access to diverse mountain landscapes within Spain's Ávila province, situated in the Sierra de Gredos Regional Park. The terrain features abundant forests of oaks, pines, and chestnuts, intersected by rivers like the Arenillas and Tormes. Glacial erosion has shaped the granite rocks, creating distinctive lagoons, cirques, and gorges, with the majestic Almanzor peak dominating the scenery. Best hiking trails around San Martín Del Pimpollar The most popular hiking route is Las Chorreras de San Martín del Pimpollar Trail, a 6.8 miles (10.9 km) moderate trail that takes 3 hours 6 minutes to complete, featuring waterfalls, Roman bridges, and poplar groves along streams. Another top favourite among local hikers is San Martín del Pimpollar — circular by Cerro Redondo, a moderate 7.0 miles (11.2 km) path that offers a circular route around Cerro Redondo. Local hikers also love the El Puerto del Pico from San Martín del Pimpollar — circular through the Sierra de Gredos (GR-10), a 13.0 miles (21.0 km) trail leading through the Sierra de Gredos, often completed in about 6 hours. Hiking around San Martín Del Pimpollar is defined by dense forests, glacial formations, and granite rock landscapes, with a network of routes offering options for various ability levels. The routes in San Martín Del Pimpollar are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 140 reviews. More than 1000 hikers have used komoot to explore San Martín Del Pimpollar's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available around San Martín del Pimpollar?
A: There are over 65 hiking routes around San Martín del Pimpollar, offering a wide range of options for different skill levels. These include 13 easy routes, 40 moderate routes, and 12 difficult trails, ensuring there's something for everyone.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on the hiking trails in San Martín del Pimpollar?
A: The terrain around San Martín del Pimpollar is characterized by diverse mountain landscapes within the Sierra de Gredos Regional Park. You'll encounter abundant forests of oaks, pines, and chestnuts, granite rock formations shaped by glacial erosion, and paths that often follow rivers like the Arenillas and Tormes. Some paths may require sure-footedness.

Q: Are there any family-friendly hiking options in San Martín del Pimpollar?
A: Yes, San Martín del Pimpollar offers several family-friendly hiking options. There are 13 easy routes that are generally suitable for families. While specific routes aren't detailed as 'family-friendly' in the data, the presence of natural swimming pools and picturesque bridges along trails like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1343103081/las-chorreras-de-san-martin-del-pimpollar-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Las Chorreras de San Martín del Pimpollar Trail&lt;/a&gt; can make for an engaging experience for children.

Q: What natural features and viewpoints can I discover on hikes in this region?
A: Hiking in San Martín del Pimpollar offers stunning natural features. You can explore beautiful waterfalls and cascades like Las Chorreras del Pimpollar and Cascada del Cerecedo. The region also boasts impressive viewpoints such as Puerto del Risquillo and Serranillos Pass, which provide panoramic vistas of the Tormes Valley, the Gredos massif, and other mountain ranges. The Sierra de Gredos Regional Park itself features lagoons, cirques, and dramatic gorges, with the majestic Almanzor peak visible from many points.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in San Martín del Pimpollar?
A: The region is generally excellent for hiking, with summer offering refreshing dips in natural swimming pools and waterfalls like Las Chorreras del Pimpollar. Spring and autumn provide pleasant temperatures and vibrant landscapes. Winter hiking is also possible, especially for those seeking a more challenging experience amidst the powerful display of the gorges.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes available?
A: Yes, there are several circular hiking routes. A popular option is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1343130660/san-martin-del-pimpollar-circular-by-cerro-redondo" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;San Martín del Pimpollar — circular by Cerro Redondo&lt;/a&gt;, a moderate 11.2 km path. Another notable circular trail is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1343148798/el-puerto-del-pico-from-san-martin-del-pimpollar-circular-through-the-sierra-de-gredos-gr-10" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Puerto del Pico from San Martín del Pimpollar — circular through the Sierra de Gredos (GR-10)&lt;/a&gt;, which is a more difficult 21.0 km route.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about the trails in San Martín del Pimpollar?
A: The hiking trails around San Martín del Pimpollar are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 140 reviews. Hikers often praise the diverse mountain landscapes, the abundant forests, and the stunning natural features like waterfalls and glacial formations. The well-established routes and varied terrain are also frequently highlighted.

Q: Are there any historical or cultural attractions near the hiking trails?
A: Beyond natural beauty, the area offers cultural and historical points of interest. You can find several ancient Roman bridges, such as the Puente de la Cacera, crossing local rivers. The 16th-century Gothic church, Iglesia de San Martín Obispo, is a notable example of local heritage. Additionally, the villages of San Martín del Pimpollar and Navalsauz preserve charming traditional architecture.

Q: Where can I find parking for hiking trails in San Martín del Pimpollar?
A: While specific parking locations for each trail are not detailed, San Martín del Pimpollar is a hub for outdoor activities, suggesting accessible parking options within or near the town. For routes starting directly from the town, local parking facilities would be the most convenient. For trails deeper in the Sierra de Gredos Regional Park, designated parking areas like the 'Plataforma de Gredos' are available for access to specific high-mountain routes.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the hiking trails in San Martín del Pimpollar?
A: The Sierra de Gredos Regional Park, where San Martín del Pimpollar is located, generally welcomes dogs on trails, though they should typically be kept on a leash to protect wildlife and ensure the safety of other hikers. It's always advisable to check specific regulations for individual routes or protected areas within the park before heading out with your dog.

Q: Are there any easy hiking trails suitable for beginners?
A: Yes, there are 13 easy hiking routes around San Martín del Pimpollar, perfect for beginners or those looking for a relaxed walk. These trails offer a gentle introduction to the region's beautiful landscapes without significant elevation gain or technical difficulty.

Q: What are some of the notable mountain passes or viewpoints in the area?
A: The region is home to several impressive mountain passes and viewpoints. You can visit &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2544555" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Serranillos Pass&lt;/a&gt;, known for its beautiful vistas of the Cinco Villas Valley and the Gredos peaks. Other passes include &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2544560" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puerto de Pedro Bernardo&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2684507" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Centenera Pass&lt;/a&gt;, all offering expansive views of the surrounding natural environment.
tips: In the foothills of the Ávila mountain range, between Puerto de Menga and Puerto de Pico you reach the charming town of San Martín del Pimpollar. The Alberche River and the small bridges are an incredible sight to discover.
Section of the GR-10 between Navarrevisca - Puerto El Pico in the foothills of the northern mountain range of Gredos. This rural land is beautiful, quiet and desolate.
The trail south of the town of San Martín del Pimpollar reaches an excellent spot for wild swimming. Are you brave enough to get your toes wet?
Crossing the old bridge, over the Arenilles River, when you enter the small town of San Martín del Pimpollar from the south. The bridge is believed to have been originally built during Roman times.

faqs.question: Where can I park and access the Las Chorreras de San Martín del Pimpollar Trail?
faqs.answer: The trail conveniently starts and ends in the village of San Martín del Pimpollar. You can typically find parking within the village itself, making it easily accessible for hikers.

faqs.question: Is the Las Chorreras de San Martín del Pimpollar Trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: Yes, the trail is generally considered dog-friendly. However, it's always recommended to keep your dog on a leash, especially since you might encounter cattle farms along the route and to respect the natural environment of the Sierra de Gredos Regional Park.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to hike this trail?
faqs.answer: The trail offers different experiences depending on the season. In summer, the streams and waterfalls provide refreshing spots, and the weather is generally pleasant. In winter, the landscape can be spectacular with ice and snow, though conditions might be more challenging. Spring and autumn offer mild temperatures and beautiful colors.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or entrance fees required for this trail or the Sierra de Gredos Regional Park?
faqs.answer: No, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to hike the Las Chorreras de San Martín del Pimpollar Trail or to access this part of the Sierra de Gredos Regional Park. However, always respect local regulations and leave no trace.

faqs.question: Is the route suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: Yes, the trail is largely considered to have a low to moderate difficulty level. With an elevation gain of around 250 meters over its 10.9 km distance, it's suitable for various fitness levels and features mostly accessible paths, making it a good option for beginners looking for a scenic hike.

faqs.question: Is this a loop trail or an out-and-back route?
faqs.answer: The Las Chorreras de San Martín del Pimpollar Trail is a circular route, meaning it starts and ends in the same location, allowing you to experience varied scenery without retracing your steps.

faqs.question: What natural features and landmarks can I expect to see along the way?
faqs.answer: The main attraction is undoubtedly Las Chorreras themselves, beautiful waterfalls formed by local streams, especially the prominent Chorrera de San Martín del Pimpollar. You'll also cross several picturesque bridges, including the Stone Bridge of San Martín del Pimpollar and the Roman Bridge of San Martín del Pimpollar, and enjoy views of the Sierra del Arenal and the broader Sierra de Gredos.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on the route?
faqs.answer: The terrain is varied and generally easy to access, consisting of well-marked natural paths, as well as some paved and unpaved sections. You'll walk through areas of broom, cattle farms, brambles, poplar groves, green meadows, and pine forests, often following the course of streams and rivers.

faqs.question: Does the trail intersect other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, portions of this route follow ancient transhumance paths and intersect with longer hiking trails. You'll find sections that are part of the GR-10 Trail: Navarrevisca to Puerto El Pico, as well as parts of 'A vueltas con Gredos' and 'De Valencia a Lisboa (Etapa 33)'.

faqs.question: Are there any amenities or places to eat near the trailhead?
faqs.answer: Since the trail starts and ends in San Martín del Pimpollar, you'll find basic amenities within the village. It's a charming village that offers glimpses into local history, including the 16th-century Romanesque Church of San Martín Obispo, and likely has options for refreshments or a meal after your hike.

faqs.question: Is the trail suitable for mountain biking?
faqs.answer: Yes, the Las Chorreras de San Martín del Pimpollar Trail is noted as being cyclable, making it suitable for mountain biking in addition to hiking. Its moderate difficulty and varied terrain can offer an enjoyable ride.
